Agent.sig: A configured, runnable agent produced by agent.

AgentConfig.sig: The declarative configuration of an agent, passed to agent (or a harness-bound factory).

AgentFactory.sig: A Deps-bound agent constructor: call it with an AgentConfig to get an Agent.

AgentSpec.sig: A const <id> = agent({ … }) declaration. Field order mirrors core's AgentConfig.

AnyTool.sig: type AnyTool<Deps> = { description: string; examples?: readonly JsonValue[]; inputSchema: StandardSchemaV1<unknown, unknown>; name: string;…

ApprovalDecision.sig: type ApprovalDecision<I> = | { kind: "approve"; } | { kind: "reject"; message: string; } | { input: I; kind: "edit"; };

ApprovalRequest.sig: type ApprovalRequest = SuspensionRequest<"tool.approval", { input: JsonValue; name: string; }, ApprovalDecision<JsonValue>>;

asTool.sig: function asTool<In, ChildDeps, COut>(child, opts): Tool<string, In, COut, unknown>;

AsToolOptions.sig: Options for asTool: how a sub-agent is exposed as a callable tool.

ChatAgent.sig: The minimal agent shape createChatStore needs: stream(history) yielding a run's events.

ChatMessage.sig: A single chat turn — structurally an InputMessage, so the history feeds straight back into a run.

ChatRequest.sig: The provider-agnostic semantic input for one model call.

ChatSnapshot.sig: An immutable view of a chat: completed messages, the in-flight assistant streaming text, and status.

ChatStore.sig: A useSyncExternalStore-compatible chat store with a send action. Created by createChatStore.

Checkpointer.sig: The persistence contract for durable runs.

CheckpointRecord.sig: One persisted checkpoint in a run's history — an opaque run token plus routing metadata.

classifiedError.sig: function classifiedError( name, message, cls, opts?): SerializedError;

CodeRegion.sig: Verbatim TypeScript preserved losslessly through spec→code→spec round-trips.

CodeResult.sig: type CodeResult = | { logs: readonly string[]; ok: true; value: unknown; } | { error: string; logs: readonly string[]; ok: false; };

CodeRunner.sig: A runtime-agnostic seam for running a snippet of code and capturing its result and logs.

ContextMeter.sig: A projection of a run's accounting for the inspector's cost/context meters.

ContiguityResult.sig: type ContiguityResult = | { ok: true; } | { missingFrom: number; ok: false; };

createApp.sig: function createApp( template, appName, dir): Promise<readonly string[]>;

createHarness.sig: function createHarness<Deps>(): { agent: AgentFactory<Deps>; plugin: <Tools>(p) => Plugin<Deps, Tools>; tool: ToolFactory<Deps>; };

CustomEventOf.sig: type CustomEventOf<Id, P> = EventMeta & { payload: P; type: `custom.${Id}`; };

DepsOption.sig: type DepsOption<Deps> = [Deps] extends [void] ? { deps?: undefined; } : { deps: Deps; };

DevtoolsHandle.sig: The handle returned by every mount function: push new options in, or tear the view down.

devtoolsPlugin.sig: function devtoolsPlugin(inspector?): Plugin<unknown, readonly AnyTool<unknown>[]> & { inspector: Inspector; };

DownloadReport.sig: A model-download progress report, mirrored from @mithril/providers/transformers's

DraftEvent.sig: type DraftEvent = | { payload: JsonValue; type: `custom.${string}`; } | Omit<EventOf<"tool.repair">, keyof EventMeta> | Omit<EventOf<"tool.…

EngineChunk.sig: type EngineChunk = | { kind: "token"; text: string; } | { kind: "reasoning"; text: string; } | { callId?: string; input: JsonValue; kind: "…

EngineRequest.sig: The semantic request handed to a TransformersEngine (a flattened ChatRequest).

EntryMessage.sig: A chat message in an entry input.

EntrySpec.sig: What the generated file executes: await run(<target>, <input>).

EventConsumer.sig: A passive observer that receives every MithrilEvent in order.

EventMeta.sig: The envelope fields present on every MithrilEvent.

EventTransport.sig: A gap-detecting, cross-runtime event bus built on web standards only.

FileSystem.sig: The rooted, async filesystem contract shared by every @mithril/fs adapter.

FinalizeCall.sig: The structured-output finalize unit wrapped by a Middleware.finalize handler: the model's final

FinalizeOutcome.sig: type FinalizeOutcome = | { status: "ok"; value: JsonValue; } | { issues: JsonValue; status: "invalid"; };

FsTestAdapter.sig: Test-runner adapter that lets fileSystemConformance run under any framework.

GenAiSpan.sig: A reconstructed OpenTelemetry GenAI span, following the gen_ai.* semantic conventions.

HandoffSuspension.sig: type HandoffSuspension = SuspensionRequest<"handoff.suspended", { child: SuspensionDescriptor; to: string; }, JsonValue>;

healing.sig: const healing: { argRepair: <Deps>() => Middleware<Deps>; defaults: <Deps>() => readonly Middleware<Deps>[]; harmonyRepair: <Deps>() => Mid…

type InferPluginTools

= P extends (...args) => infer R ? InferPluginTools : P extends Plugin ? { [E in T[number] a…

InputMessage.sig: type InputMessage = | { content: string; role: "user"; } | { content: string; role: "assistant"; };

InspectedRun.sig: A captured run: its id, ordered event log, replayed RunState, and TimelineEntry projection.

JsonSafe.sig: type JsonSafe<T> = T extends JsonValue ? T : never;

JsonValue.sig: type JsonValue = | null | boolean | number | string | readonly JsonValue[] | { [k: string]: JsonValue; };

Keyring.sig: A source of signing/verification keys for sealed tokens, indexed by key id (kid).

KeyValue.sig: A runtime-agnostic async key-value store (§10.2) for tools — caches, dedup sets, counters, scratch state.

KvTestAdapter.sig: Minimal test-runner shim that lets kvConformance register cases against bun:test / vitest without

LiveProvider.sig: A remote provider a browser snippet can call directly with the user's own key.

LocalModel.sig: A curated in-browser model — all text-generation ONNX repos. This MUST hold: the transformers

LogLine.sig: One forwarded console.* line from the snippet.

LoopOptions.sig: The full set of inputs to agentLoop — the flattened, already-resolved form of an

makeRunHandle.sig: function makeRunHandle<Out>( gen, runId, controls): RunHandle<Out>;

McpClient.sig: A connected MCP client over an McpTransport. Create one with mcpClient.

mcpServer.sig: function mcpServer( tools, info?, opts?): McpServer;

McpServer.sig: A Mithril-tools-backed MCP server. Create one with mcpServer.

McpServerInfo.sig: Identifies an mcpServer to connecting clients.

McpToolDef.sig: An MCP server's description of one tool, as returned by tools/list.

mcpTools.sig: function mcpTools(client): Promise<readonly Tool<string, JsonValue, JsonValue, unknown>[]>;

McpTransport.sig: The transport you implement to carry MCP JSON-RPC calls to a server.

Message.sig: One conversation message with any tool calls it issued.

Middleware.sig: Producer-side composability: observe or transform a run purely by wrapping

MiddlewareContext.sig: The context handed to a Middleware — a subset of RunContext

MithrilEvent.sig: type MithrilEvent = | EventMeta & { depsDigest: string; input: JsonValue; model: string; type: "run.start"; } | EventMeta & { reason: Finis…

ModelCall.sig: The model-call input a Middleware.model wrapper observes or transforms.

ModelCapabilities.sig: The feature flags a model supports, used for capability-gated routing.

ModelResult.sig: The result of one model invocation seen by a Middleware.model wrapper.

ModelSpec.sig: The published spec for one model: its capabilities, pricing, and context size.

narrow.sig: function narrow<Tools>(e, tools): e is EventMeta & { callId: string; input: JsonValue; name: string; type: "tool.call"; version?: string } …

OpaqueDecl.sig: A top-level statement the parser did not recognize as a framework-shaped declaration. Kept

open.sig: function open( token, keyring, rt?, opts?): Promise<string>;

ParseDiagnostic.sig: One parser message, with source offsets a code editor can mark.

parseProject.sig: function parseProject( source, ts, prev?): ParseResult;

PendingSuspension.sig: The serialized description of what a suspended run is waiting on — enough to resume it in another

Plugin.sig: A bundle of tools, middleware, and event consumers registered as a unit.

PluginHost.sig: The registration surface passed to a Plugin.setup, for contributing plugin fragments.

Pricing.sig: Per-token pricing for a model, in integer micro-USD, matching UsageDelta's cost fields.

ProjectDecl.sig: type ProjectDecl = | ToolSpec | AgentSpec | SubAgentToolSpec | WorkflowSpec | OpaqueDecl;

ProjectSpec.sig: A whole serializable project: an ordered list of declarations plus the entry to run.

Provider.sig: A model provider: its ProviderSpec plus a streaming chat entry point.

ProviderChunk.sig: type ProviderChunk = | { delta: string; type: "text.delta"; } | { delta: string; type: "reasoning.delta"; } | { callId: string; name: strin…

ProviderRegistry.sig: Resolves a ModelId to its Provider over the set of registered ProviderSpecs.

ProviderSpec.sig: A provider's published spec: its id and the models it offers keyed by name.

ResolutionOf.sig: type ResolutionOf<R> = R extends SuspensionRequest<string, JsonValue, infer T> ? T : never;

resolveModel.sig: function resolveModel(model, registry?): { id: `${string}/${string}`; provider: Provider; };

ResumeDirective.sig: Resume a previously suspended run from a persisted token instead of starting fresh.

ResumeState.sig: The reconstructed loop state driving a resume, assembled by agent from a run token.

ResumeValue.sig: type ResumeValue = | ApprovalDecision<JsonValue> | { kind: "resolve"; value: JsonValue; };

RunContext.sig: The typed dependency-injection context threaded into tools and dynamic

RunHandle.sig: A live handle over a streaming run, returned by Agent.stream.

RunInspectorOptions.sig: Options for mountRunInspector. Provide exactly one of events / source / transport.

RunnerClient.sig: Owns the runner worker and exposes the run's accumulated state.

RunnerMessage.sig: type RunnerMessage = | { event: MithrilEvent; type: "event"; } | { level: LogLevel; text: string; type: "log"; } | { info: SuspendedInfo; t…

RunnerRequest.sig: type RunnerRequest = | { code: string; env?: Readonly<Record<string, string>>; resume?: ResumeDirective; type: "run"; } | { decision: Resum…

RunnerSnapshot.sig: The accumulated state of the current (or last) run.

RunOptionsBase.sig: The run options common to every agent, independent of whether it has dependencies.

RunResult.sig: type RunResult<Out> = | { output: Out; status: "completed"; usage: UsageTotals; } | { request: SuspensionDescriptor; status: "suspended"; t…

RunSnapshot.sig: An immutable view of a run, recomputed on every event.

RunSource.sig: Anything carrying a live event stream — a RunHandle satisfies this.

RunState.sig: The materialized state of a run — always the pure fold of its event log.

RunStore.sig: A useSyncExternalStore-compatible store over a run's events. Created by createRunStore.

RuntimeAdapter.sig: The single ambient-capability seam: every non-deterministic or platform

RunTokenV2.sig: The versioned, serializable run token carried by a suspended RunResult.

SchemaRegistry.sig: Resolves a resolutionSchemaId to its Standard Schema for validating a resume resolution.

SchemaSpec.sig: A schema stored as zod SOURCE (e.g. z.object({ city: z.string() })), not JSON Schema.

seal.sig: function seal( body, keyring, rt?, codec?): Promise<string>;

SealCodec.sig: A payload transform applied before signing (encode) and after verifying (decode) in the seal envelope.

SerializedError.sig: A JSON-safe serialized form of a thrown error, suitable for the wire.

SpanNode.sig: A node in the span tree: one span, the events stamped to it, and its child spans.

SpanRef.sig: Identifies the tracing span an event belongs to, forming the parent/child

SpanSink.sig: Receiver for reconstructed spans — implement this to bridge toGenAiSpans to a real OTel exporter.

SpecMeta.sig: Studio-only presentation data — codegen ignores it entirely.

splitToolCalls.sig: function splitToolCalls( tokens, fmt, reasoning?): AsyncGenerator<EngineChunk>;

StandardSchemaV1.sig: The Standard Schema v1 contract — a validator-agnostic interface implemented

StepInput.sig: The input to one step (model call + tool execution), observed by a Middleware.step wrapper.

StepOutcome.sig: The summary of one completed step seen by a Middleware.step wrapper.

StepResult.sig: type StepResult<S> = | { goto: string; state: S; } | { done: true; state: S; };

StepSnapshot.sig: A per-step view yielded by Agent.iterate: the step index, the events emitted during it, and a

SubAgentToolSpec.sig: A const <id> = asTool(<agentId>, { … }) declaration — a sub-agent exposed as a parent's tool.

Suspend.sig: The marker value a tool's execute returns to request a replay-free mid-tool pause.

SuspendedInfo.sig: A pending human-in-the-loop suspension, as posted by the worker: the JSON-safe

SuspensionDescriptor.sig: type SuspensionDescriptor = { callId?: string; kind: string; payload: JsonValue; resolutionSchemaId?: string; toolVersion?: string; };

SuspensionRequest.sig: A request to pause a run until a human (or external system) supplies a

TestAdapter.sig: A tiny test-runner bridge so the conformance kit runs under bun:test or

TimelineEntry.sig: One row of a run's timeline: the event's sequence, timestamp, type, and owning span id.

toGenAiSpans.sig: function toGenAiSpans( events, sink?, opts?): readonly GenAiSpan[];

Tool.sig: A typed tool: Standard Schema in/out plus typed dependency injection.

ToolCallFor.sig: type ToolCallFor<Tools> = { [T in Tools[number] as ToolNameOf<T> & string]: T extends Tool<infer N, infer In, infer _O, infer _D> ? { callI…

ToolCallRecord.sig: A single tool call and its (eventual) output within a Message.

toolCallTurn.sig: function toolCallTurn( name, input, callId?): readonly ProviderChunk[];

ToolDef.sig: The definition object passed to tool to declare a single tool.

ToolFactory.sig: A Deps-bound tool constructor: call it with a ToolDef to get a fully typed Tool.

ToolFormat.sig: How one model family delimits + encodes tool calls in generated text.

ToolInputOf.sig: type ToolInputOf<T> = T extends Tool<infer _N, infer In, infer _O, infer _D> ? In : never;

ToolInvocation.sig: type ToolInvocation = { callId: string; input: JsonValue; name: string; version?: string; };

ToolOutcome.sig: type ToolOutcome = | { callId: string; output: JsonValue; status: "ok"; } | { callId: string; error: SerializedError; status: "error"; };

ToolOutputOf.sig: type ToolOutputOf<T> = T extends Tool<infer _N, infer _In, infer Out, infer _D> ? Out : never;

ToolProgress.sig: An intermediate value yielded by a streaming tool's execute generator, surfaced as tool.progress.

ToolStepOutcome.sig: A per-tool result summary surfaced on StepOutcome.toolOutcomes, so a step-altitude healing

TransformersEngine.sig: The injected inference engine — the seam that keeps the provider testable without WebGPU/ONNX.

TransformersHandleOptions.sig: Options for transformers: EdgeOptions plus an optional injected engine (tests / custom runtimes).

Transport.sig: type Transport = | { apiKey: string; baseUrl?: string; headers?: HeadersInit; kind: "byok"; } | { baseUrl: string; headers?: HeadersInit; k…

UsageDelta.sig: Token counts and cost for a single accounting event.

UsageTotals.sig: A running usage accumulator: a UsageDelta plus a completed-step count.

UseChatResult.sig: The value returned by useChat: the current ChatSnapshot plus a send action.

useObject.sig: function useObject(source): { partial: JsonValue | undefined; value: JsonValue | undefined; };

VectorMatch.sig: A single nearest-neighbour hit returned by VectorStore.query, ordered by descending score.

VectorRecord.sig: A record written to a VectorStore: an id, its embedding, and optional JSON-safe metadata.

VectorsTestAdapter.sig: Minimal test-runner shim so vectorsConformance runs under bun:test / vitest with no hard dependency.

VectorStore.sig: A runtime-agnostic vector store (§10.4) — the portable core of retrieval-augmented generation.

withJsonSchema.sig: function withJsonSchema<In, Out>(schema, jsonSchema): StandardSchemaV1<In, Out>;

Workflow.sig: A compiled workflow. Call Workflow.run with an initial state to execute it.

WorkflowRoute.sig: type WorkflowRoute = | { goto: string; } | { done: true; };

WorkflowStepSpec.sig: type WorkflowStepSpec = | { agentId: string; assign: CodeRegion; inputExpr: CodeRegion; kind: "agentStep"; name: string; next: WorkflowRout…
